blocks.content: Everyone's eyes turned to the sky.
The black sword spun, blocking the sun, and then plunged into the ground behind Rai with a thud, embedding itself more than halfway.
Rai didn't look back.
His gaze remained fixed on Rian.
Rian, frozen in a stance with his greatsword aimed, couldn't move a finger.
The aftermath of his divine transcendence brought excruciating pain.
In the prolonged silence, Rai clicked his tongue and turned away.

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: I lost. Let's stop.
Rian's eyes widened in shock.
It was the first time in 18 years he had heard Rai admit defeat.
But Rai showed no emotion as he pulled out the black sword and sheathed it.
He walked over to Clump and bowed.

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: I lost. I admit defeat.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Heh, you got caught up in his pace. But it was nice to see you so fired up for once.

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: This is why you shouldn't get involved with fools.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: But it wasn't so bad, right? Don't you feel a bit relieved?
Rai muttered with a smirk.

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: This is why you shouldn't get involved with fools.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: But it wasn't so bad, right? Don't you feel a bit relieved?
After a moment of thought, Rai muttered as he passed Clump.

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: ...I feel a bit more like a swordsman now.
Reina quickly followed Rai.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Wait, come here. Is your hand okay? You have a deployment tomorrow, right?

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: It's nothing. I just cramped and dropped my sword.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Who are you trying to fool? Do swordsmen cramp that easily? Let me see your hand.
Reina grabbed Rai's wrist and examined his hand.
The calluses, now as tough as skin, showed that he hadn't reached this point on talent alone.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Just as I thought.
Reina frowned as she looked at the back of Rai's hand.
The left hand, which cushioned the impact when gripping the sword, had lost all its fingernails on the index, middle, and ring fingers.

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: Don't make a fuss. Fingernails fall off easily.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: I've never heard that! Ugh, you won't even be able to cut steak like this. Come here, let me bandage it.
Reina took a bandage from the first aid kit in the training ground and tightly wrapped Rai's fingers.
As the tension eased, even the spectators felt the fatigue.
But Kuan still looked at Rian with a serious expression.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Divine transcendence...

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Rian's swordsmanship in real combat was indeed impressive. It was a performance that could never be shown in the step-by-step evaluations of the swordsmanship school.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: But I would have taken a different approach.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Divine transcendence was dangerous. It wasn't just about increasing power.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: The movement that bit Rai at the end was so peculiar that it would have been difficult to create even with external force. Yet Rai responded to Rian's strong sword with his own.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Was it pride? Lack of experience? Or were there other variables on the battlefield that he hadn't read?

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: A swordsman is a technician who handles the sword.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: General.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: But a sword is ultimately a tool for killing. That's an undeniable truth. So why do we pursue the sword?

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: A single sword can never change the world. But the sword in one's heart is different. A great swordsman wins battles, but a knight with conviction changes the world. Rai was ignited by Rian's conviction.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: The sword in one's heart...

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Perhaps divine transcendence is the embodiment of conviction.
Kuan recalled a time long ago.
Before his Achilles tendon was severed, his spirit had been so strong that he chose death over accepting the Wind Guards offer. Now, that determination felt like a distant memory.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: I once had it too.
Clump looked at Kuan with kind eyes.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: How long will you wander? Return to the military. My direct subordinate is retiring. If you take the position, it would be a great help.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: I'll think about it. Thank you for your concern.
Kuan was sincere, but he had no intention of returning to the military.
Today's duel had made it clear. He was already a broken sword. Without embracing a new sword, he could never reach divine transcendence.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Grandfather, can I drop out now?
Rian approached with determined strides, his face covered in cold sweat from enduring the pain.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Yes, you won. Do as you wish, whether it's dropping out or going on a knight's quest.

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: Don't end up dead somewhere. If you die, I'll have to go looking for you.
Contrary to everyone's expectations, Rian didn't get angry.
They had both given their all, so there was no point in dwelling on the duel.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: But one question remained.
Rai paused while buttoning his shirt and turned around.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Hey, let me ask you something.

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: The technique that exploded the ground, that's an application of swordsmanship, right?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: So what?

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: Why didn't you use swordsmanship on me? You could have broken my sword and ended the duel.
Rai tilted his head in confusion.
Was Rian mocking him?
But Rian's expression showed no hint of provocation.

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: Is he serious?
Rai looked at Clump.

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: Didn't you tell Rian?

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Heh, what could I do? He took it like a thief. I hadn't even properly chosen a successor. And the Schema opening was a lie too.

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: You never intended to give it to me anyway.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: You didn't need it.

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: Tch!
Feeling left out of the conversation, Rian interrupted.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: What are you talking about? Just answer me. Why didn't you use swordsmanship?
Rai, annoyed, turned towards the exit of the training ground.

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: Don't ask me, ask Grandfather. It's a mess. A swordsman who doesn't even know what he's fighting with.
Reina followed Rai.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Are you really leaving today?

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: No, I'll stay the night. I'm tired. Bring my meal to my room.
As Rai walked away, Rian glared and pointed at him.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Hey! Where are you going? Why didn't you use swordsmanship? Don't leave me hanging!

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: There's no cure for stupidity.
Ignoring Rian's shouts, Rai pondered.

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: The Ozent family had both black-haired and blue-haired lineages.

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: Why hadn't Grandfather given him the greatsword?

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: As a child, he thought it was because Grandfather favored Rian, who had the same blue hair. But after today's duel, he had a vague understanding.

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: At least, the rightful owner of that sword was Rian.
Shirone and Tess decided to stay at Reina's mansion for the night.
The three gathered in Rian's room, catching up on the past six months.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: How's Amy doing? She must be really upset.
They had read about Amy's failure in the Spirit Journal. The article had shockingly covered the news of the Carmis family's prodigy failing early in the graduation exam.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: She was down for a while, but she's okay now. She'll definitely have a different result next year.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Yeah, Amy will get through it. I miss her. Those were fun times.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Amy deserves to be a magician. Tell her I'm rooting for her. I'll be there to cheer her on for the next graduation exam.
A year later, Shirone would also take his graduation exam, and Rian wouldn't miss it.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: You're lucky. I can't go. The swordsmanship school ends later than the magic school. Maybe I should drop out too and go on a quest with Rian.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: What's this about a top student at the swordsmanship school dropping out? You should graduate at the top of your class.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: But it feels so empty without you. I really want to go with you.
Seeing Tess on the verge of tears, Rian quickly looked for a topic to change the subject.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Shirone, are you carrying a sword now?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Oh, that's a gift.
Shirone explained what had happened in Kazra.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Since Rian and Tess had also experienced the journey to Heaven, they understood the concept of an object.
Intrigued, they urged Shirone to demonstrate.
First, Shirone performed some tricks with precise control, then activated the Vajra Armor.
The sword enveloped Shirone, and an organic robe formed around him.
The eerie yet powerful appearance left even the bold Rian speechless.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: What is this orb?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That's a magic computation device. It replaces a technique called time-sharing, making calculations much faster. And the crystal on the gauntlet is a magic amplification device. Using these two, I can expand the range of my magic.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: That's amazing. I didn't know objects could have such complex functions.
Armand was a weapon that could tempt any swordsman.
But his friends showed no interest beyond curiosity.
For those who held the sword of conviction, any tool was just a tool.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: I wish Rian could use Armand.
A knight's quest was on a different level from the swordsmanship school's curriculum.
The world wouldn't provide Rian with a systematic difficulty.
He could easily handle third-rate thugs or robbers, but some criminals were so skilled that even the kingdom struggled with them.
In that sense, Armand, which enhanced the user's physical abilities and had its own defensive capabilities, would be helpful.
However, unlike in Dante's case, the magic sword did not respond to Rian.
Armand provides the optimal combat environment for all users, but to achieve 100% efficiency, a high level of tactical understanding and complex system control is essential.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Rian's straightforward and forceful nature made it difficult for him to fully utilize Armand's functions, and Armand seemed to recognize this.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: It's really like a sword, no matter how you look at it.
But Rian had no intention of relying on anything.
His confidence was sky-high after his duel with Rai.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: No need to worry! This is just the beginning! From today, I'll achieve an undefeated record!
Shirone smiled contentedly as he watched Rian proudly thump his chest.
He was like a fire. One day, he would become a great blaze that would set the world alight and return to him.
That night.
Rian lay curled up under his blanket, groaning.
Ugh! Ugh!

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: The aftermath of divine transcendence didn't end with the first wave.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: The most intense pain came at night when his body was recovering.
Groans escaped his throat, but he bit his pillow to endure.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: More than anything, he feared that if he screamed now, he would never be able to endure it again.
His face was drenched in cold sweat, and his eyes were unfocused.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Yet, Rian fiercely stared into the darkness of the night, enduring the pain.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Thank goodness. I'm so glad I won.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: If he had lost to Rai in front of Shirone, he would have fallen into despair and been unable to do anything for a while.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Rai was undoubtedly strong. In terms of technical perfection, he was worthy of being called a genius.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: But he wasn't an insurmountable wall.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: The gap has narrowed. It definitely has.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: He couldn't predict that the same result would come from another duel, but in some aspects, he had certainly surpassed Rai.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: He was confident in that.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: I can do it. I didn't choose the wrong path.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Just knowing that the distance wasn't unreachable filled Rian with joy.
Even as his body trembled with pain, laughter escaped his throat.
Heh, heh heh heh.
The night of pain and pleasure passed.
